data.title: Why a Lone Hold Rating Appeared on Alibaba (BABA) Before Earnings
data.description:
E-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) is expected to report Q3 FY26 results this month. While most Wall Street analysts remain bullish on the stock ahead of its upcoming results, one firm is stepping to the sidelines. Erste Group analyst Hans Engel downgraded Alibaba from Buy to Hold, becoming the only analyst in the latest coverage cycle without a Buy rating on the stock.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move comes even as sentiment around Alibaba has improved due to its AI and cloud momentum.

Profitability Is the Real Concern

The analyst said Alibaba's AI strategy is a positive. Management is focused on generating revenue from AI through its cloud business. The company is also developing its own AI chips, which over time could make Alibaba less dependent on outside suppliers and strengthen its platform.

These efforts may improve the company's competitive position and support future growth as more businesses increase spending on AI computing.

That said, the analyst's main concern is profitability.

He noted that Alibaba's operating margin has declined in recent quarters. Heavy spending on technology and infrastructure, including AI projects, is weighing on earnings. The company's long-term liabilities have also increased, which could limit flexibility if growth slows.

As a result, he does not expect a sharp earnings recovery soon, even if sales remain stable. For now, he believes the stock is likely to trade sideways rather than deliver strong gains.

What Is the Price Target for BABA Stock?  

According to TipRanks, Alibaba stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 15 Buys and one Hold rating assigned in the last three months. The average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.38% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: DE Earnings: Deere Stock Leaps on Strong Q1 Beats
data.description:
Deere (DE) stock rallied on Thursday after the farm equipment company released its Fiscal Q1 2026 earnings report. The company started the report off strong with diluted earnings per share of $2.42. While this was down 24% year-over-year from $3.19, it still came in above Wall Street's estimate of $2.02 per share.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Deere reported revenue of $9.61 billion in its latest earnings report, which represents a 13% increase from the $8.51 billion reported in Fiscal Q1 2025. The company's revenue also surpassed the analysts' estimate of $7.59 billion for the quarter. Deere's revenue growth was fueled by a 3% increase in Production & Precision Ag net sales and a 24% increase in Small Ag & Turf net sales.

DE stock was up 5.69% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 1.26% rally yesterday. The shares have soared 27.43% year-to-date and 19.66% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Microsoft Stock Is Triggering a Bull Run as a 110% Surge in AI Contracts Creates a $625 Billion Revenue Stream
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is aggressively reclaiming its spot at the top of the tech world. While the stock faced a brief 15% slide earlier this year, the underlying numbers are now grabbing the market's attention. The company's total cloud revenue has soared past $51 billion, and the future earnings look even stronger. Microsoft has secured a staggering $625 billion in signed contracts that have yet to be billed. This massive wall of revenue represents a massive flood of guaranteed cash for the company.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Massive Backlog Creates a $625 Billion Engine of Growth

The Remaining Performance Obligation (RPO) is the single most dramatic number in Microsoft's portfolio. It represents over half a trillion dollars in guaranteed business waiting to hit the balance sheet. This figure exploded by 110% in the most recent update, largely fueled by its deep partnership with OpenAI. Microsoft is now the primary landlord for the world's most advanced AI research. Expecting to recognize $156 billion of that backlog as revenue within the next year gives the company a level of visibility and security that is almost unheard of in the tech sector.

Copilot Adoption Drives Growth

For the first time, Microsoft revealed that 15 million people are now paying for Microsoft 365 Copilot. While this represents 160% growth year-over-year, it is only the beginning of the story. Currently, only 3.3% of Microsoft's 450 million commercial users have upgraded. This low adoption rate fuels the bull case by leaving a massive runway for future profits. Converting just a small fraction of its remaining 435 million users could add billions in revenue without ever needing to find a new customer.

Microsoft's Strategic Spending Builds an Unbreakable Lead

The debate over Microsoft's $37.5 billion quarterly spending is shifting from risk to reward. CEO Satya Nadella has made it clear that the company is currently supply-constrained, meaning it cannot build data centers fast enough to keep up with customer demand. Building out custom AI chips and global infrastructure now ensures that Microsoft remains the primary landlord of the AI era. With AI service growth currently at 157%, the market is starting to view this spending as a necessary foundation for the next decade of dominance.

Is Microsoft a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, MSFT stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Microsoft stock price target is $593.38, suggesting a potential upside of 48.5% from the current level.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 'An Important Potential New Option:' Merck Stock (MRK) Slides despite Successful RSV Trial
data.description:
Drugmaker Merck & Company's (MRK) shares edged lower in Thursday's pre-market trading despite reporting positive results from its late-stage SMART clinical trial for severe respiratory syncytial virus (RSV).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New Jersey-based pharma company said its study showed similar safety results for children under the age of two at risk of RSV who were given its injection, enflonsia (scientifically called clesrovimab), both during the first and second RSV seasons.

RSV is a common winter virus that causes airway and lung infections among infants. It is counted among the leading causes of infant hospitalization globally.

Merck's enflonsia is a lab-made antibody designed to protect high-risk infants and children through five months, a typical RSV season. Typically, infants are administered protective shots monthly throughout each season.

However, the Merck trial aimed to test the efficacy of enflonsia when an extra shot is given at the start of the second RSV season. In June 2025, the U.S. health watchdog approved the medication for a single season, and the drug has also secured approval in Canada and other countries for single-season usage.

"These new data from the SMART study further position enflonsia as an important potential new option to help protect young children who remain at high-risk entering their second RSV season,"  noted Dr. Macaya Douoguih, a vice president at Merck Research Laboratories.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Down Over 30% This Year: Analysts Double Down on These 3 Beaten-Down Stocks
data.description:
What should you do with a stock that's been falling? Some would say, sell it now and cut your losses – but others say, look for opportunities. There are a myriad of reasons why a stock can fall, but Wall Street's analysts will tell you that you need to read those reasons with care.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In some cases, the decline reflects genuine deterioration – weakening fundamentals, shrinking demand, or a business model that no longer fits the market. Those are red flags investors need to recognize. In other instances, however, the pressure stems from temporary setbacks: a softer product cycle, tougher competition, or broader market volatility that sweeps up even solid companies.

The real challenge is distinguishing between structural problems and short-term turbulence. No one can consistently time the exact bottom, but identifying quality names that have been repriced can tilt the odds in your favor.

With that in mind, we turned to the TipRanks database to screen for stocks that have dropped sharply yet still get the backing of Wall Street analysts. Each of the three names below is down at least 30% so far in 2026, yet the analyst community remains constructive. Let's take a closer look at why the analysts are doubling down.

Unity Software (U)

First on our list here is Unity Software, founded in 2004 in Denmark but currently based in San Francisco. Unity offers its users a software platform for creating video games – and not just to create them, but also to expand them, to gather an audience, to create an interactive experience, and to make it available across all major mobile, PC, console, and XR platforms. In short, Unity plays a foundational role in a major segment of the computing industry.

A short trip through Unity's website shows that the company can offer a wide range of services, including 2D and 3D game creation, user acquisition apps so that creators can bring in an audience, and monetization apps that allow creators to generate revenue from their work. In addition, the Unity platform has applications in a wide range of non-gaming industries, including immersive training, retail, manufacturing, customer experience, and 3D training. Unity's systems are compatible with both augmented and virtual reality devices, through designer and user interfaces.

Unity's own users have access to a range of interactive connections, including livestreams and discussions, to get the most out of the platform by finding out what other users are doing with it. It's part of Unity's overall commitment to providing transparency in the service.

In its near-quarter century of operation, Unity has carved out a clear niche and built itself into an ~$8 billion company. In the second half of last year, Unity started to see its stock rise – but that trend reversed itself sharply in the last few weeks. The stock is now down 58% year to date.

The company is facing headwinds from the AI industry, where mega-cap firms like Google are taking the lead in AI and interactive world generation. In a more recent headwind, Unity's revenue outlook for 1Q26, reported last week, came in with a range of $480 million to $490 million, well below the $494 million expected on Wall Street.

The soft outlook overshadowed a solid report. In 4Q25, Unity's top line came to $503 million, up 10% year over year and beating the forecast by $10.18 million. The company's bottom line, of 24 cents per share by non-GAAP measures, was 3 cents per share better than expected.

Oppenheimer analyst Martin Yang, in his recent note on Unity, explains why the stock remains a sound opportunity despite the headwinds. He says, "The recent sell-off, driven by fears that 'world models' like Google's Project Genie will displace game engines, is fundamentally misplaced and ignores the distinct architectural role Unity plays in development. We view the current valuation as disconnected from the company's improving fundamentals, specifically the successful re-acceleration of the Grow segment via the Vector, and disciplined cost management. We view the current risk/reward profile as favorable. We believe U will see accelerating revenue growth and margin expansion in 2026, validating its role not as a victim of AI, but as a primary platform for its deployment."

Putting this into quantifiable terms, Yang rates the stock as Outperform (i.e., Buy), with a $32 price target that implies an upside of 72.5% on the one-year horizon. (To watch Yang's track record, click here)

The 20 analyst reviews on file for Unity include 15 to Buy, 4 to Hold, and 1 to Sell, giving the stock its Moderate Buy consensus rating. U shares are trading for $18.55, and the $37.53 average target price indicates potential for a robust 102% one-year upside. (See  Unity Software stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Alibaba Stock Forecast Ahead of Q3 Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
E-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) is set to report fiscal Q3 2026 results soon. The stock rose more than 24% in 2025 and roughly 6% so far this year, helped by demand for its AI services and steady cloud growth. Still, tougher local competition, heavy spending that may pressure margins, and slower trends in its core retail business remain key risks.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Expectations from Alibaba 

For Q3 FY26, Wall Street analysts expect earnings of $1.68 per share, down from $3.09 in the year-ago quarter. Revenue is projected at about $42.0 billion, representing an 8% year-over-year increase.

During the earnings call, investors will focus on cloud revenue and overall sales growth. Markets will look for continued strength in Alibaba's AI and cloud demand, along with stable e-commerce performance. They will also watch margins as the company increases AI spending. 

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Ahead of results, Erste Group analyst Stephan Lingnau downgraded Alibaba from Buy to Hold. He noted that the company is increasingly focused on making money from AI through its cloud business and by building its own AI chips, which is a positive. However, he also pointed out that operating margins have dropped and long-term debt has increased. Because of these mixed trends, the analyst believes the stock is likely to "move sideways in the medium term" rather than post strong gains.

Meanwhile, Jefferies analyst Thomas Chong reaffirmed a Buy rating and a $225 price target, saying Alibaba is well placed for the rise of AI agents. The 5-star analyst pointed to the latest Qwen app update, which now works closely with Alibaba's main services such as Taobao, Alipay, Fliggy, and Amap. This lets users shop, order food, plan trips, and make payments in one place.

Chong said this shows how Alibaba is turning AI into a tool that completes tasks, not just answers questions. He also highlighted strong momentum at Alibaba Cloud, noting that many business customers already use both AI models and computing services. With most companies still early in AI adoption, Jefferies sees a long runway for cloud growth ahead. 

What Is the Price Target for BABA Stock?  

According to TipRanks, Alibaba stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 15 Buys and one Hold rating assigned in the last three months. The average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.38% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: SPY vs. VOO vs. IVV: Which S&P 500 (SPX) ETF Should You Buy?
data.description:
